title: add support to copying files from container
body:
Hi, I'm working on adding support for buildah in Ansible (Container): https://github.com/ansible/ansible/pull/26170

It seems like we may also need to copy files from container to host or another container. How difficult would it be to implement? Do you think that sufficient workaround could be to mount a directory instead?

comments.body:
You could simple mount the directory, We are implementing most of the upstream docker cli in kpod, so this might be a solution as well.

kpod copy.

We have buildah copy, which copies into the container.

mnt=$(builder mount CONTAINERID)
cp $mnt/PATHTOSRCE /PATHTODEST
buildah umount $mnt

Wrote a quick blog on this.
http://danwalsh.livejournal.com/76656.html

Dan, thanks for your comments. In the meantime I implemented via doing `cat` in the container and piping the output into file on host: https://github.com/ansible/ansible/pull/26170/files#diff-9119cb69870060cdff9c271b8c397222R115

Edit: btw I like the mount/umount solution, I'll use it. Let's close then. Thank you!!
